title: Ophelia umbellata
taxonomy: Ophelia umbellata is classified within the family Sapindaceae, which consists of over 130 genera of flowering plants. It is a member of the order Sapindales and the class Magnoliopsida. This plant is further classified as a dicotyledon, characterized by its two cotyledons or seed leaves. Within the Sapindaceae family, Ophelia umbellata is closely related to other plants like Aesculus and Acer. Further research is needed to confirm the exact classification within the family Sapindaceae.
uses: Ophelia umbellata has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever and respiratory issues. The plant's leaves and roots are believed to possess antimicrobial properties, which can help combat infections. In some cultures, Ophelia umbellata is also used to treat digestive problems and as a diuretic. However, the efficacy of these uses has not been extensively scientifically studied. Further research is needed to confirm the plant's medicinal properties.

parts: Ophelia umbellata, also known as the creeping cinquefoil, is a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States.
